01
What we see when we actually look
The Photos & The Merch

A few people in Lewisburg — some in campers, some not — started taking photos. Of the town. Of the businesses. Of the leaves at Coon Range in autumn.

"One usually LOOKS before one listens.
Thus the name: Lewisburg Looks."

Leaves of Lewisburg Cardigan — autumn leaves from Coon Range, reworked with William Morris wallpaper influence. $65. Available from one Lewisburg location (to be announced).

Coon Range Hat — offered to the Coon Range Fish and Game Club board years ago. They did not look at the pictures and ended the meeting. We hope they see this as a general plus for Coon Range.

Leaves of Lewisburg Vest — same leaf pattern, vest form.

Lewisburg Ghetto Bowler Skullsock — for collecting buttons. The people of Lewisburg will also be getting (or buying) pins with local businesses and organisations on them — think of kids with band pins on their denim jackets. We're appreciating our ma-and-pop businesses like rock stars. 🤘 QR code goes to our Smartphone Aversion Deconstructive Detox playlist. QR code goes to our Smartphone Aversion Deconstructive Detox playlist. Map of Russellville — where we currently go — but we'd like to stay here more.

Logan Likes / Logan Listens Canvas Tote — carry your music with you. These totes and the Ghetto Bowler Skullsock work beautifully with our playlist pins — QR code buttons linking directly to Logan Likes and Logan Listens.

We put band pins on our denim jackets for the bands we love. Why not for the places we love? The pins feature our ma‑and‑pop businesses and local organisations — the same star that lives in the PanderThing’s P, made visible and wearable. Lewisburg’s real stars, on your lapel. Your imagination, gently poked.

Troutvis — coming soon. A canvas of a Lewisburg business or organisation, made to hang in another local establishment. Named after Kilgore Trout: a picture doing more work than a thousand words, and funnier. We are champing at the bit to make the first one. In the meantime, the art you see here is a fair indication of what we’re capable of.

★  OUR LOCOMPROTION  ★
Iron Mountain Honey  —  Mr. Larry Vaught

Raw wildflower honey from Dunmoor, Lewisburg, Kentucky. Mr. Vaught’s bees nectar from willow, maple, and the wildflowers of Logan County’s fields. He actively seeks wild bees from the area for cultivation.

(270) 977-5823  ·  larryvaught2015@gmail.com

02
A mini-pilgrimage around Lewisburg
The Filgrimpidge

We sell different items at different places around Lewisburg. Not everything at one place. The idea is to get people walking around a bit — visiting these places that are dear to us, seeing each other, talking.

"A Walter Benjamin experiment in aura.
You have to come.
That's the point."

An object that can only be purchased in one specific place, from one specific person, carries something with it that an object purchased online does not. Walter Benjamin called it aura. The filgrimpidge is how we put that theory into practice at the scale of one small Kentucky town.

1Lewisburg location TBA  Coon Range Hat, Cardigan, Vest
2Other participating establishments  Totes, buttons, other merch — to be confirmed
★Everywhere  Buttons with QR codes linking to Logan Likes & Listens playlists

We hope several establishments will agree to sell some of these items — earning a profit of course. Each stop on the filgrimpidge is a reason to visit a local business you might not have visited otherwise. That's the whole game.

Not all items are available in all places. That's not a bug. That's the aura working.

★  OUR LOCOMPROTION  ★
Iron Mountain Honey  —  Mr. Larry Vaught

Mr. Larry Vaught trains and conducts the Lewisburg Community Choir on Sunday afternoons. He teaches children music and tunes pianos. He has brought Lewisburg’s pastors and ministry leaders together for unity and collaboration.

He knows the value of quiet and prayer more than most.

03
Local. Co-promotion. A little loco.
Locomprotion & Open Hearts

When someone walks into Business A, they see something about Business B. Business D features canvases showing Business A. The businesses are in visual conversation with each other before anyone has had to sit through a networking lunch.

"Expanding imagination
through cheap gossip."

Say something nice about a competitor. Watch what happens. "The other day we went to Harry's BBQ — AND — OH HEAVEN LICK MY TOES!! Their FRIES!!" This looks real because it is real.

Open Hearts, Open Hands — brings together churches for food pantry work. They also pray with you. If life is hard right now: worth visiting more than our merch is.

Iron Mountain Honey — Mr. Larry Vaught. Honey from local beehives in Dunmoor. Trains the Lewisburg Community Choir, brings pastors together, tunes pianos. An extraordinary person.

(270) 977-5823 · larryvaught2015@gmail.com · facebook.com/IronMountainHoney

★  OUR LOCOMPROTION  ★
Iron Mountain Honey  —  Mr. Larry Vaught

A Kentucky State bee advisor came to visit Mr. Vaught’s wildest, nastiest bees — which also produce wildly, nastily good honey. Iron Mountain Honey: raw wildflower honey from Logan County.

larryvaught2015@gmail.com  ·  facebook.com/IronMountainHoney

04
The envelope, gently expanded
Logan Likes & Logan Listens

Logan Likes — pop music with something to do with Logan County. Music you might already know.

Logan Listens — historic jazz, classical, experimental music from Kentucky artists. The cultural envelope, gently expanded.

"Start with what people know.
Then: and here is something
you might not know yet."

Supported by graphic buttons with QR codes at local businesses. Pick one up. Scan it. Hear something new. Your imagination: gently poked.

★  OUR LOCOMPROTION  ★
Iron Mountain Honey  —  Mr. Larry Vaught

Mr. Vaught knows a lot about what people are like when they’re together — from teaching the kids, from training the choir (notorious gossips, every one!), and maybe especially from watching the bees.

facebook.com/IronMountainHoney  ·  (270) 977-5823

05
Coming soon — if things go well
Lewisburg Looks Gazette

A monthly list of events happening in Lewisburg and the surrounding area. One page. Five cents. Available on paper only — at local ma and pop businesses and churches.

"Get the people coming outside,
seeing each other,
visiting these places that are dear to us."

It will also have an online component to help businesses and organizations plan their events — so there aren't big lulls, and nothing overlaps unnecessarily.

The Gazette is a teaser. It's what becomes possible if things go well with everything else. We mention it now so people know what they're helping to build toward.

Interested in Lewisburg Looks? Contact Mr. BlackSocks: teach.the.conflicts@gmail.com or text (704) 728-0885 — text first with a good time to call.

★  OUR LOCOMPROTION  ★
Iron Mountain Honey  —  Mr. Larry Vaught

Teaching sixth graders at the Muhlenberg County Agriculture & Convention Center. Getting Logan County’s children excited about beekeeping, local wildflowers, and the quiet patience it takes to tend something living.

(270) 977-5823  ·  Lewisburg, KY

06
Invented here, with a fake bird
Get a PanderThing

A PanderThing is a clicky-movey promotional HTML document you email to people as an attachment. Party in front, business in back. Invented right here in Lewisburg with the help of a fake bird — the Ibis, to be specific. The Ibis rises from the ashes with each new conversational thread, inspired by Plato's story of Thoth and Thamus. Mr. BlackSocks and the Ibis: a fine team, if we do say so ourselves.

"Promoting stuff is harder
than it looks.
A PanderThing makes it easier."

Every PanderThing also includes the PanderThing Alternative File for Properly Skeptical Folk — a plain word processor version for people with entirely justified suspicions about zip attachments.

We strongly encourage you to include a good word for another Logan County business. Say something nice about a competitor. This is locomprotion. It costs nothing. It helps everyone.

First few are free for businesses that help spread the word. Text (704) 728-0885 first with a good time to call. Email: teach.the.conflicts@gmail.com
